body:
Title: CRA Form YT428 and Worksheet YT428 2025 Yukon resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5011-c/5011-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5011-c/5011-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5011-d/5011-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5011-d/5011-d-25e.pdf

CRA Form YT428 2025 calculates Yukon tax for Yukon residents. Part A calculates Yukon tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $57,375, $114,750, $177,882, and $500,000. The rates are 6.4%, 9%, 10.9%, 12.8%, and 15%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $3,672.00, $8,835.75, $15,717.14, and $56,948.24.

Part B calculates Yukon non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is capped at $16,129. Line 58080 age amount is capped at $9,028. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $2,000. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,834 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 6.4%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 6.4% and line 14 by 12.8%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Yukon non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Yukon tax. Line 52 adds tax on taxable income and tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet YT428 line 61520 calculates the dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 0.67% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 12.02%. Line 55 multiplies minimum tax carryover by 44.14% and line 58 multiplies Form T691 Part 7 line 14 by 43.9%. Lines 63 to 70 calculate the business investment tax credit using certificate and unused amounts, capped at $25,000 and by line 62 tax, and calculate unused credit. Lines 71 to 73 request carrybacks of unused business investment tax credit. Line 75 subtracts the Yukon First Nations income tax credit. Worksheet YT428 line 78 calculates the political contribution credit using thresholds of $400, $750, and more than $1,275, rates of 75%, 50%, and 33.33%, base credits of $300 and $475, and a maximum credit of $650. Line 79 enters Yukon tax on line 42800.
